description: An electrical discharge in the atmosphere is 'pheluka', often seen as a sudden flash within a cloud or between cloud and ground. 'horuma' (thunder) is the sound produced by rapidly heated air around the discharge. Cloud and distance can hide either sign from an observer.
articulatory_notes: Bring the lips close together for unvoiced bilabial 'ph' and let the narrow breath enter middle-height front 'e'. Touch the tongue tip to the alveolar ridge for lateral 'l' and release into stressed high-back rounded 'u'. Close the back of the tongue against the soft palate for 'k', then open into central 'a'.

semantic_domains.nature: an electrical discharge within storm weather
semantic_domains.physical: a discharge that produces light, heat, and expanding air
